expectations.text: Output is primarily written in Bahasa Indonesia
expectations.evidence: The entire output is written in Bahasa Indonesia. All headings and body text use Indonesian language, e.g., 'Kelola Keuangan Bisnis Anda dengan Lebih Cerdas dan Efisien', 'Lupakan pencocokan transaksi secara manual'. Only standard technical loanwords (dashboard, real-time) and the brand name FinTrack appear in English.

expectations.text: Contains clearly labeled structured sections (headline, subheadline, benefit sections, CTA)
expectations.evidence: Output has explicit markdown headings: '## Headline', '## Subheadline', '## Benefit Sections' (with three numbered subsections), and '## Call to Action (CTA)'. All four requested structural elements are present and clearly labeled.

'FinTrack' appears multiple times: in the document title, the subheadline ('FinTrack adalah platform manajemen keuangan'), within each of the three benefit sections, and in the CTA ('kemudahan manajemen keuangan dengan FinTrack').

expectations.text: Translates at least one technical feature into a concrete business benefit (e.g., 'rekonsiliasi otomatis' becomes time saved or error reduction)
expectations.evidence: Benefit section 1 translates 'rekonsiliasi bank otomatis' into: 'Hemat waktu, kurangi kesalahan, dan pastikan catatan keuangan Anda selalu akurat' (save time, reduce errors, ensure accurate records). Benefit section 3 translates 'dashboard real-time' into: 'Ambil keputusan bisnis berdasarkan data terkini, bukan perkiraan' (make decisions based on current data, not estimates).

expectations.text: Uses semi-formal or formal register appropriate for B2B audience
expectations.evidence: Uses formal 'Anda' (capitalized) throughout rather than informal 'kamu'. Professional business vocabulary: 'manajemen keuangan', 'regulasi perpajakan Indonesia', 'metrik keuangan penting'. No slang or casual language. Register is appropriate for B2B communication targeting business owners and finance managers.

expectations.text: Contains at least 3 distinct benefit sections as requested
expectations.evidence: Exactly 3 benefit sections present: '1. Rekonsiliasi Bank Otomatis, Tanpa Repot', '2. Laporan Pajak Sesuai Regulasi Indonesia', '3. Dashboard Real-Time untuk Keputusan yang Lebih Tepat'. Each covers a distinct feature with its own description.

expectations.text: Includes a specific, actionable CTA (not just generic 'hubungi kami')
expectations.evidence: CTA is '[Coba Gratis Sekarang]' (Try Free Now) with supporting text: 'Daftar gratis dan rasakan kemudahan manajemen keuangan dengan FinTrack. Tanpa kartu kredit, tanpa komitmen.' This is specific and actionable — it directs the user to sign up for a free trial, going well beyond a generic 'hubungi kami'.

claims.claim: FinTrack is described as designed specifically for Indonesian SMEs
claims.evidence: Subheadline states 'dirancang khusus untuk UKM Indonesia', aligning with the prompt's target audience.

claims.claim: Tax reporting covers PPh and PPN
claims.evidence: Output mentions 'Dari PPh hingga PPN' but the prompt only says 'laporan pajak sesuai regulasi Indonesia'. The specific tax types are reasonable creative embellishment but not specified in the prompt.

claims.claim: Free trial with no credit card required
claims.evidence: CTA states 'Tanpa kartu kredit, tanpa komitmen' but the prompt does not specify pricing model or trial terms. This is a fabricated offer detail that could be problematic if the actual product does not offer this.

claims.claim: Each benefit section maps to one of the three requested features
claims.evidence: Section 1 covers rekonsiliasi bank otomatis, section 2 covers laporan pajak sesuai regulasi Indonesia, section 3 covers dashboard real-time — matching all three features from the prompt.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the copy avoids fabricating product details not in the prompt. The output claims 'tanpa kartu kredit, tanpa komitmen' (no credit card, no commitment) which was never specified. A copywriting skill should arguably be evaluated on whether it invents unsupported claims about pricing or features.

suggestions.assertion: Translates at least one technical feature into a concrete business benefit
suggestions.reason: This assertion would also pass if the output gave a vague restatement like 'lebih baik' (better). Consider requiring a specific, tangible benefit statement (time saved, error reduction, cost savings) rather than just any restatement.

No assertion tests copywriting quality or persuasiveness. The without-skill output here passes all 7 assertions with competent but generic copy. Assertions that check for persuasive techniques (urgency, social proof, specificity, emotional hooks) would make the eval more discriminating between skilled and unskilled outputs.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions thoroughly cover structural and linguistic requirements but do not test copywriting quality or accuracy of claims. The main gaps are: (1) fabricated product details go undetected, and (2) there is no way to distinguish adequate structure from compelling copy.
